item.title: NextEra-Dominion mega-deal throws spotlight on US power M&A rush
item.description: As demand grows and supply tightens amid regulatory and supply chain bottlenecks, investors are increasingly seeking out operational and late-stage development assets

item.title: UK to curb judicial reviews that stall giant wind farm projects
item.description: Government set to restrict use of key legal weapon used against major renewable energy and grid projects

item.title: Seatrium VP: Integration key to reaching industrial scale in offshore wind
item.description: Aziz Merchant warns Recharge Wind Power Summit that fragmented designs and supply chains inflate costs and delays as offshore wind races toward 20MW-plus turbines

item.title: German wind group seeks 16GW return option to avoid offshore gridlock
item.description: Proposal would affect massive capacity secured by TotalEnergies, BP and others and avoid delays that could affect projects worth up to $58bn, BWO calculates

item.title: One key cost uncertainty could stall Asia's wind boom, warns finance chief
item.description: Finance chief at OCBC Bank – a major renewables lender – said that little-discussed charge can stop otherwise bankable wind power projects in their tracks

item.title: Orsted shakes-up UK top team at 'pivotal moment' for offshore wind
item.description: Danish group names new country manager as industry veteran Sykes takes new role

item.title: TotalEnergies says German wind group behind idea to give up offshore sites
item.description: French oil & gas major insists it continues to 'actively work' on German projects, but confirms proposal on return of areas is under discussion within offshore wind association BWO

item.title: Bank warns Southeast Asia wind and solar 'execution gap' is threat to regional growth
item.description: Key markets such as Vietnam, Thailand and Indonesia seeing high renewable energy project cancellations as investors come up against inadequate policies and grids, says Standard Chartered

item.title: Germany's offshore wind auction model faces reality check as TotalEnergies balks
item.description: A $14.7bn bet on German seabed rights now looks shaky with TotalEnergies apparently seeking an exit or better terms for massive wind zones

item.title: CIP boss warns governments over offshore wind strategy
item.description: Danish offshore wind pioneer said governments must react to 'unforeseen circumstances' but developers must not take advantage of flexibility afforded

item.title: Singapore and Japan launch floating wind pact as Recharge summit kicks off
item.description: Partnership announced at the inagural Recharge Wind Power Summit in Singapore aims to bring together city-state’s offshore energy experience with Japan’s floating offshore wind expertise

item.title: TotalEnergies wants to give back giant German offshore wind sites: report
item.description: French oil & gas giant in offshore wind auctions secured 7.5GW in North Sea sites in 2023 and 2024

item.title: NextEra CEO who slated offshore wind gets largest US project under $67bn deal
item.description: John Ketchum says 'it's the right thing to do to finish it' when asked about 2.6GW project underway by merger partner Dominion

item.title: Seven ways investors get floating wind wrong
item.description: Investing in floating wind can be a quick way to lose money if project backers make any of seven key errors that have sunk projects previously

item.title: NextEra and Dominion agree $67bn mega-deal to form new wind and utility giant
item.description: Combined group will have 110GW of generation in deal reflecting scale of challenge to meet data centre demand

item.title: Suzlon chief on new Europe push: 'We're not here to undercut rivals'
item.description: Indian wind turbine maker has got its mojo back after several difficult years and is angling for European orders once again

item.title: Asia's offshore wind ambitions hinge on bankable auctions after Europe's costly lessons
item.description: APAC governments must design bankable auctions or risk repeating Europe’s offshore wind delays; signs are positive after an oversubscribed tender in South Korea

item.title: Record deal to merge NextEra and Dominion would create new wind giant
item.description: Agreement would address demand for data centre power and create enlarged wind power force

item.title: One problem, three answers – RWE, Equinor and Iberdrola
item.description: How Europe's three largest energy groups are reshaping capital around the same problem deploying new renewables capacity

item.title: The all-seeing project that could help unlock Japanese floating wind
item.description: Ocergy's design is already at the heart of two key floating wind projects in Europe and this California company with a distinctly French flavour is about to achieve an unusual entry into the Japanese market

item.title: VIDEO: Orsted installs first monopile at Hornsea 3 offshore wind farm
item.description: Watch the first of 197 XXL monopiles being installed on Orsted’s Hornsea 3 offshore wind farm in the UK North Sea. The XXL monopiles are the largest used by Orsted on any of its European wind farms to date and each will be topped by a 15MW turbine. They were installed using Cadeler’s Wind Ally installation vessel. (Video credit: Orsted)

item.title: Chinese wind turbine makers sweep global top five for first time
item.description: China-based OEMs even more dominant at top of rankings thanks to huge domestic onshore wind market and increasing ambitions abroad, says Global Wind Energy Council

item.title: CIP leads the pack as Korea's latest offshore wind auction attracts bumper capacity
item.description: Korean renewables tender could provide some interesting answers on the future of wind turbine manufacture and assembly in the region

item.title: Deals of the Week: Envision, Nexans, Iberdrola, Mubadala
item.description: China's globetrotting wind and storage giant opens a new market as a clutch of big names in renewables engage in M&A agreements

item.title: Strong policy, weak delivery: Statkraft boss on where UK and Ireland fall short on renewables
item.description: Statkraft is building across wind, solar, batteries and grid-stability projects in the UK and Ireland, where rising renewables penetration is forcing faster progress on storage, hybrid projects and system integration

item.title: Two huge UK offshore wind projects win planning consent
item.description: Offshore wind farm being developed in two phases in North Sea by RWE and Masdar could power three million UK homes annually

item.title: Orsted hints at renewed appetite for growth in key APAC markets including South Korea and Australia
item.description: Leading renewables developer said measures taken to strengthen capital structure and financial foundations mean the company is ready to pursue new 'value-creating opportunities' within offshore wind

item.title: Mingyang boss reveals new plan to win orders in Europe
item.description: Leading offshore wind turbine supplier believes models designed in Europe will be ‘much easier’ to sell internationally

item.title: Offshore wind vs data centres: battle for capital is on, says key bank
item.description: European sector needs to broaden lender base and carefully structure financing to meet 'intense competition' from other infrastructure segments, says Japan's MUFG

item.title: Interior chief attacks permitting 'logjam' while defending Trump's wind and solar roadblocks
item.description: Interior secretary Doug Burgum defended administration's blocking of renewables while lamenting 'red tape' that is holding back $1.5trn in energy investment
